behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in multi-module state handoff (git-derived branch state + age filter + PR presence), so unit-only can miss wiring bugs (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System: behavior text does not require live HTTP/CLI contract validation or explicit round-trip external side-effect proof; it only requires correct classification logic across components.
behaviors.test_description: Create an integration test that wires the real staleness detector, branch-state loader, and PR-state adapter with realistic fixture data (branches ahead of main, timestamps beyond threshold, no open PR) and assert stale classification is true only when all three conditions hold.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is bootstrap orchestration between startup flow, staleness signal, and warning renderer. Unit tests of formatter/decision function alone can miss missing hook registration or dropped context fields (MOCK-MISS). Rejected System: behavior does not explicitly require real OS/session manager contract checks or external readback beyond in-process emitted warning.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initialization in a test harness with a stale branch state injected through real internal wiring, and assert one warning event/message is emitted containing required branch context fields (branch name, age/commit status, and action hint).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function with no I/O or cross-module dependency. Higher levels add little new reality signal for this specific mapping rule.
behaviors.test_description: Use table-driven unit tests for classifier inputs to assert exact output labels for representative branch states and edge cases.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo and PR state (REAL-INFRA) and rendered externally visible output based on real round-trip reads. Mocked repo/PR clients can hide real contract or data-shape issues (MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real repository context with real PR state (or real API-backed test repo), then assert rendered output shows `clean` for non-stale branch and `STALE` for branch ahead of main with no open PR.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is correct combination of branch staleness logic with PR-status interpretation across modules. Integration catches mapping/wiring regressions that unit-only misses. Rejected System: behavior text does not require live external API contract validation; it can be validated with integrated components plus controlled PR-status fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire staleness evaluator with PR-status provider in an integration test and verify branches tied to open or merged PR statuses are classified non-stale-no-pr even when other stale indicators are present.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash correctness depends on real git history behavior with changed commit hashes, typically exercised via real git subprocess/repo operations (REAL-INFRA). In-process fakes often miss graph/ancestor edge cases (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly calls out rebase/squash hash-change scenarios that are infrastructure-accurate only with real VCS operations.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create stale branch, then perform actual rebase/squash-merge flows that rewrite hashes; run staleness detection end-to-end and assert branch is no longer reported as unPRed stale once changes are represented in main history.
